channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.114
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.114 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Shared Agent Context: How We Are Tackling Partner Agent Collaboration
item.description: Your Azure SRE agent detects a spike in error rates. It triages with cloud-native telemetry, but the root cause trail leads into a third-party observability platform your team also runs. The agent can't see that data. A second agent can, one that speaks Datadog or Dynatrace or whatever your team...
item.dc:creator: dbandaru

item.title: "No-Ops" Kafka Experience: Why Event Hubs is Your Default Destination for Streaming on Azure
item.description: For many engineering teams, the promise of real-time data streaming is often overshadowed by the "operational tax" of maintaining the infrastructure. Managing an Apache Kafka® cluster traditionally means wrestling with broker patches, Zookeeper (or KRaft) orchestration, disk rebalancing, and the...
item.dc:creator: ashish-chhabria

item.title: GitHub Copilot for Jira — Public preview enhancements
item.description: Since launching the public preview of GitHub Copilot coding agent for Jira, we’ve been listening closely to customer feedback. Thank you to everyone who has taken the time to try… The post GitHub Copilot for Jira — Public preview enhancements appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Cosmos DB Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric with private endpoints
item.description: We're excited to announce that private endpoint support for Azure Cosmos DB Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric is now generally available. You can now unlock powerful analytics on your operational data while keeping your enhanced network security posture intac
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Blue-green agent pool upgrade in AKS
item.description: In‑place node pool upgrades can introduce risk by applying changes directly to running environments. Blue‑green agent pool upgrades create a parallel node pool with the new configuration, allowing validation before workloads are shifted and providing a cl
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Updates to GitHub Copilot interaction data usage policy
item.description: From April 24 onward, interaction data—specifically inputs, outputs, code snippets, and associated context—from Copilot Free, Pro, and Pro+ users will be used to train and improve our AI models unless they opt out. The post Updates to GitHub Copilot interaction data usage policy appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Mario Rodriguez

item.title: Updates to our Privacy Statement and Terms of Service: How we use your data
item.description: Hey GitHub Community, We’ve made some important updates to our Privacy Statement and Terms of Service to keep you informed about how we handle your data. Notably, from April 24… The post Updates to our Privacy Statement and Terms of Service: How we use your data appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now identify active Copilot coding agent users
item.description: Copilot usage metrics now indicate which users have Copilot coding agent (CCA) activity. Enterprise and organization admins can identify which users are actively using Copilot coding agent on daily and… The post Copilot usage metrics now identify active Copilot coding agent users appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Take Control of Every Message: Partial Failure Handling for Service Bus Triggers in Azure Functions
item.description: The Problem: All-or-Nothing Batch Processing in Azure Service Bus Azure Service Bus is one of the most widely used messaging services for building event-driven applications on Azure. When you use Azure Functions with a Service Bus trigger in batch mode, your function receives multiple messages at...
item.dc:creator: swapnil_nagar

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Fabric Mirroring integration for Azure Database for MySQL
item.description: We are excited to announce the public preview of Fabric Mirroring integration for Azure Database for MySQL – Flexible Server. You can now replicate MySQL operational data into Microsoft Fabric in near real time, without building or maintaining ETL pipelin
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure SQL Managed Instance change event streaming
item.description: You can now stream row‑level data changes—inserts, updates, and deletes—from Azure SQL Managed Instance to Azure Event Hubs in near real time with change event streaming (CES). SQL publishes changes as transactions commit, reducing latency while minimizin
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Custom time zone support for pg_cron via cron.timezone in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: You can now modify the cron.timezone server parameter in Azure Database for PostgreSQL. This parameter controls the time zone used by pg_cron when evaluating scheduled jobs. By configuring cron.timezone, you can ensure scheduled jobs run according to your
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL migration service supports compatible EDB workloads into Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: We’re excited to announce that EDB PostgreSQL is now supported as a source for migration into Azure Database for PostgreSQL. You can migrate and consolidate PostgreSQL estates from EDB Postgres Extended Server to Azure using secure, reliable workflows des
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL migration service supports for Google AlloyDB into Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: We’re excited to announce that Google AlloyDB is now supported as a source for migration into Azure Database for PostgreSQL. You can migrate and consolidate PostgreSQL estates from Google AlloyDB to Azure using secure, reliable workflows designed for mini
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Online migration now uses the pgoutput plugin
item.description: You can now leverage pgoutput for online (minimal-downtime) migrations with improved reliability and performance. By aligning with PostgreSQL’s native logical replication framework, this update improves ecosystem compatibility with modern PostgreSQL deplo
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Disable comments on individual commits
item.description: Repository admins can now disable comments on individual commits, a small but meaningful improvement for maintainers dealing with unwanted noise on old commits. A new “Commits” section in repository settings… The post Disable comments on individual commits appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Redefining Database Maintenance after Migrating from Db2 on Mainframe to Azure SQL DB Hyperscale
item.description: Introduction Migrating from Db2 z/OS to Azure SQL Database Hyperscale is a major step toward modernizing your mainframe relational data infrastructure to Azure Data SQL managed relational database offering. But what happens to all those daily and periodic Db2 database maintenance tasks you used to...
item.dc:creator: Sandip-Khandelwal

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.113
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.113 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Azure Managed Redis & Azure Databricks: Real-time Feature Serving for Low-Latency Decisions
item.description: This blog content has been a collective collaboration between the Azure Databricks and Azure Managed Redis Product and Product Marketing teams. Executive summary Modern decisioning systems, fraud scoring, payments authorization, personalization, and step-up authentication, must return answers in...
item.dc:creator: Jason_Pereira

item.title: Identity security is the new pressure point for modern cyberattacks
item.description: Read the latest Microsoft Secure Access report for insights into why a unified identity and access strategy offers strong modern protection. The post Identity security is the new pressure point for modern cyberattacks appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rob Lefferts and Nadim Abdo

item.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot app learning series: Real‑world scenarios for your workday
item.description: If you’ve heard more about Microsoft 365 Copilot and wondering where to begin, you’re not alone. With AI becoming part of everyday work, many people are looking for guidance that’s practical, approachable, and grounded in real workflows—not just feature lists. That’s why we’re excited to introduce...
item.dc:creator: Patrick_Gan

item.title: Aspire on Azure App Service is now Generally Available
item.description: Today we are announcing General Availability of Aspire on Azure App Service, making it easier to take distributed applications from local development to a fully managed production environment on Azure App Service. With the Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppService package, you can define your hosting...
item.dc:creator: TulikaC

item.title: 🚀GitHub Copilot CLI: AI Assistance from the Command Line for Infrastructure Deployments
item.description: What is GitHub Copilot CLI? GitHub Copilot CLI is an AI-powered assistant that runs directly inside your terminal. Instead of manually writing commands, scripts, or debugging issues, you can simply describe what you want — and Copilot executes it. Here’s the shift: Traditional CLI Copilot CLI You...
item.dc:creator: ibrahimbaig

item.title: v1.14.8
item.description: 1.14.8 (March 25, 2026) BUG FIXES: Prevent crash in the display of relevant attributes after provider upgrades (#38264)
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: Microsoft Foundry Labs: A Practical Fast Lane from Research to Real Developer Work
item.description: Why developers need a fast lane from research → prototypes AI engineering has a speed problem, but it is not a shortage of announcements. The hard part is turning research into a useful prototype before the next wave of models, tools, or agent patterns shows up. That gap matters. AI engineers want...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Vectorless Reasoning-Based RAG: A New Approach to Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: Introduction Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become a widely adopted architecture for building AI applications that combine Large Language Models (LLMs) with external knowledge sources. Traditional RAG pipelines rely heavily on vector embeddings and similarity search to retrieve relevant...
item.dc:creator: Rajapriya

item.title: 🚀 Securing Enterprise AI: From Red Teaming to Risk Cards and Azure Guardrails
item.description: AI is no longer experimental—it’s deeply embedded into critical business workflows. From copilots to decision intelligence systems, organizations are rapidly adopting large language models (LLMs). But here’s the reality: AI is not just another application layer—it’s a new attack surface. 🧠 Why...
item.dc:creator: adityakumar60

item.title: Closing the Last Mile in Document AI: Improving Extraction Quality in Azure Content Understanding
item.description: The Promise (and Pain) of Automated Document Processing Every enterprise runs on documents. Invoices, contracts, tax forms, medical records, insurance claims…the list never ends. Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) promises to eliminate the manual work of reading, interpreting, and entering data...
item.dc:creator: chu_lahlou

item.title: Your Entire Engineering Floor Just Stopped Coding
item.description: And the developers running Claude Code and GitHub Copilot CLI didn’t notice… Status Page Says ‘Operational.’ Your Subagents Say Otherwise. If you’re running autonomous agents in any serious capacity, you’ve experienced this: model provider outages aren’t edge cases — they’re part of the operating...
item.dc:creator: Desi Villanueva

item.title: Guidance for detecting, investigating, and defending against the Trivy supply chain compromise
item.description: Threat actors abused trusted Trivy distribution channels to inject credential‑stealing malware into CI/CD pipelines worldwide. This analysis walks through the Trivy supply‑chain compromise, attacker techniques, and concrete steps security teams can take to detect and defend against similar attacks....
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Container network metrics filtering for AKS
item.description: Network observability can generate large volumes of metrics, making it difficult for teams to focus on data that is operationally relevant. Container network metrics filtering in Azure Container Networking Services (ACNS) allows operators to control which
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: AI Agent for container networking troubleshooting
item.description: Troubleshooting Kubernetes networking issues is often slowed by logs and metrics scattered across multiple tools, forcing engineers to manually correlate signals during incidents. The container networking agent provides a lightweight, web‑based interface
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: HTTP Triggers in Azure SRE Agent: From Jira Ticket to Automated Investigation
item.description: Introduction Many teams run their observability, incident management, ticketing, and deployment on platforms outside of Azure—Jira, Opsgenie, Grafana, Zendesk, GitLab, Jenkins, Harness, or homegrown internal tools. These are the systems where alerts fire, tickets get filed, deployments happen, and...
item.dc:creator: Vineela-Suri

item.title: Ask @copilot to make changes to a pull request
item.description: You can now mention @copilot in pull requests to ask Copilot to make changes. You can ask @copilot to: Fix failing GitHub Actions workflows: @copilot Fix the failing tests Address… The post Ask @copilot to make changes to a pull request appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Manage Copilot coding agent repository access via the API
item.description: Organization owners who want to manage Copilot coding agent access at scale can now do so programmatically with the new Copilot coding agent management REST APIs, available in public preview.… The post Manage Copilot coding agent repository access via the API appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Hosted Containers and AI Agent Solutions
item.description: If you have built a proof-of-concept AI agent on your laptop and wondered how to turn it into something other people can actually use, you are not alone. The gap between a working prototype and a production-ready service is where most agent projects stall. Hosted containers close that gap faster...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: AKS managed GPU metrics in Azure Monitor
item.description: Teams running GPU‑backed workloads often lack integrated visibility into GPU utilization alongside Kubernetes metrics. AKS managed GPU metrics automatically expose performance and utilization data from NVIDIA GPU‑enabled node pools into managed Prometheus
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Cross-cluster networking in Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager
item.description: Organizations running applications across multiple Kubernetes clusters often face challenges with performance, global service discovery, and observability due to the complexity of distributed microservice environments. Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager now p
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 now with Elastic SAN integration and on demand installation
item.description: Containerized workloads often require higher and more consistent storage performance without managing large numbers of individual disks. Azure Container Storage integration with Elastic SAN allows Kubernetes clusters to consume storage from a shared pool
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Container network logs in AKS
item.description: Networking issues in Kubernetes environments can be difficult to diagnose due to limited visibility into traffic flows and insufficient context around failures. Container network logs in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), now generally available, provide con
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor Prometheus community recommended alerts for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes
item.description: Azure Monitor now offers one-click enablement of Prometheus recommended alerts directly in the Azure Portal for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes clusters. These alerts, based on enhanced Prometheus community rules, provide comprehensive coverage across cluste
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Application routing with meshless Istio in AKS
item.description: Following the deprecation of ingress‑nginx, Kubernetes operators need a supported, standards‑aligned migration path for ingress without the complexity of a full service mesh. Application Routing with Meshless Istio enables adoption of the Kubernetes Gatew
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Azure Kubernetes Application Network
item.description: As Kubernetes environments scale across regions and clusters, IP‑based networking becomes difficult to manage and provides limited application‑level visibility and security controls. Azure Kubernetes Application Network introduces application‑layer abstra
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Ingest OTLP data into Azure Monitor with the OpenTelemetry Collector
item.description: Azure Monitor now supports native ingestion of OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) signals, enabling you to send telemetry data directly from OpenTelemetry-instrumented applications and platforms to Azure Monitor. You can configure your OpenTelemetry Collector
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Governing AI agent behavior: Aligning user, developer, role, and organizational intent
item.description: This research report explores the layers of agent intent and how to align them for secure enterprise AI adoption. The post Governing AI agent behavior: Aligning user, developer, role, and organizational intent appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Fady Copty, Neta Haiby and Idan Hen

item.title: Building AI-powered GitHub issue triage with the Copilot SDK
item.description: Learn how to integrate the Copilot SDK into a React Native app to generate AI-powered issue summaries, with production patterns for graceful degradation and caching. The post Building AI-powered GitHub issue triage with the Copilot SDK appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Griffiths

item.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot Declarative Agents Are Getting Smarter with GPT‑5.2
item.description: We’re continuing to improve the intelligence behind Microsoft 365 Copilot Declarative Agents. As part of this effort, Declarative Agents are being updated to use GPT‑5.2, a newer large language model designed to deliver higher‑quality responses across complex agent scenarios. This update focuses on...
item.dc:creator: Shikha_Varshney

item.title: Unlock More Power in Your Development Workflow: Syncfusion for Visual Studio Subscribers
item.description: A few months ago, I was talking with a developer who said something that stuck with me: “I love building apps. I just don’t love rebuilding the same UI controls over and over again.” That’s the reality for a lot of teams. You want to focus on your business logic, your architecture, your...
item.dc:creator: Jim Harrer

item.title: Automating Azure OpenAI Cost Control Using Budgets, Action Groups, and Automation Runbooks
item.description: Introduction As organizations increasingly adopt Azure OpenAI for use cases such as generative AI, analytics, and experimentation, cost governance becomes a critical operational concern. Azure OpenAI workloads can scale quickly, and without proper controls, unmonitored usage may lead to unexpected...
item.dc:creator: trisha1302
